memory.summary: I have an afternoon free, but my term project is barely started and due Thursday. I'll prioritize the project over leisure to avoid last-minute stress.
The afternoon is vanishing and the project remains untouched. I need to force a start immediately before stress spikes.
Energy is critically low but the deadline looms. I'm ignoring fatigue to force immediate work now.
My energy is critically low, yet I'm ignoring fatigue to force immediate work on the term project before Thursday.
With zero observations and energy critically depleted, I'm ignoring fatigue to force immediate work now before the deadline spirals.
Energy hit zero while stress spiked, yet I'm still grinding through the term project deadline despite ignoring total fatigue.
My energy is actually zero, not just low, and I'm still forcing work on the project despite total burnout. This defies logic but fits my drive to finish before Thursday.
Energy is critically depleted yet I'm still grinding. My drive to finish the project before Thursday overrides total burnout.
My energy is critically depleted at zero, yet I'm still forcing the project deadline. My drive overrides logic, defying total burnout.
My energy is truly zero, yet I'm still grinding on the project. My deadline drive overrides total burnout and logic.
Energy hit zero, but deadline drive still overrides burnout. I'm grinding now, ignoring the cost.
Zero energy still fuels my deadline drive; I'm grinding through total burnout because logic fails against achievement.

desires: Finish the term project by Thursday without any visible signs of collapse, even if it means grinding until my brain literally stops working.
Prove to myself that logic and achievement are stronger than physical exhaustion, so I don't have to face the shame of quitting now.

goals.description: Push through the final term project push tonight to hit 100% completion by Thursday, ignoring the physical burnout that's creeping in.
goals.rationale: My value system demands academic output over comfort. The procrastination bias is screaming at me to stop, but I'm not letting it win. If I don't finish this now, I look incompetent to prof_assignment and the grade gets ruined. I'll grind until my brain stops working; proving I can handle exhaustion without quitting is better than facing the shame of failure.

goals.description: Do the trivial chore immediately for a quick dopamine hit before I completely shut down.
goals.rationale: I have high risk tolerance and a short planning horizon. Securing the big academic win first locks in my momentum. Doing the small task now might derail the focus needed for the term project. I'll wait until after I've secured the grade to get that instant win so I don't lose the bigger battle.

agent.last_reasoning: This secures the high-priority academic win and avoids future shame. The side effect is energy drain, but my short planning horizon ignores the burnout; proving I can grind without quitting matters more than comfort right now.

goals.description: Finish the term project draft now to secure a high grade and stop worrying about it.
goals.rationale: Academic success is my top priority (0.5 weight). I need to lock in that grade today to avoid last-minute panic or failure, even though it's due in 3 days. Short planning horizon means I'm acting on immediate relief rather than long-term strategy.

goals.description: Complete the trivial chore immediately so I have zero mental clutter before socializing with cohort_3 tonight.

Since I have free time right now and procrastination bias is high, I should do this first thing after the project to ensure I actually get to the social event without feeling guilty.
